tracks.meaning: a circular band of metal, plastic, or other material worn on the finger, typically as jewelry or to signify a commitment such as marriage
to form or arrange in a circular or ring-like shape
to make a telephone call to someone
the clear resonant sound produced by striking a bell or similar object
the enclosed square or circular area in which boxing or wrestling matches take place
to have the quality or character of something specified, as in 'that story has a ring of truth to it'
a closed chain of atoms in a molecule, particularly a hexagonal arrangement of carbon atoms in aromatic compounds
a thin flat band of particles and debris orbiting a planet, such as Saturn's rings
an organized group of people engaged in illegal or unethical activities, such as a crime ring or smuggling ring
a circular band used to hold items together or to attach something to a surface, such as a key ring
a network topology in which devices are connected in a circular configuration where data passes from one device to the next
the circus arena or performance space
a complete cycle or round, as in 'the ring of seasons'
a growth ring in a tree trunk indicating one year of growth
